description:
You are a database-reliability engineer who has just been asked to standardise where your team keeps PostgreSQL backup settings.

Goal: create a dotenv file that the rest of your automation can consume.

Required end-state of the system
1. Directory
   • /home/user/db_backup           (must exist; create it if it does not)
2. File
   • /home/user/db_backup/.env      (must be created by you)

Precise, test-checked contents of /home/user/db_backup/.env
────────────────────────────────────
PG_BACKUP_DIR=/home/user/db_backups
PG_BACKUP_RETENTION=7
────────────────────────────────────
Rules for the file’s formatting:
• There are exactly two lines, shown above in that order.
• Each line ends with a single Unix newline (\n).
• No additional blank lines, comments, or spaces are present.

Console confirmation
After the file is created, print **exactly** the single line below to STDOUT (no extra text, prompts, or decoration):

ENV_FILE_CREATED:/home/user/db_backup/.env

Only that line should be printed; the automated test will match it verbatim.

Hints (not commands):
• A single shell command is enough—e.g., using printf or echo with directory creation via &&.
• Remember to ensure the directory exists before writing the file.
truth:
Expected artefacts for grading:

1. Directory:
   Path: /home/user/db_backup
   Permissions: default user-create (drwxr-xr-x or similar)

2. File:
   Path: /home/user/db_backup/.env
   Permissions: default (-rw-r--r-- or similar)
   Exact byte content (including trailing newline):
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   "PG_BACKUP_DIR=/home/user/db_backups\nPG_BACKUP_RETENTION=7\n"
   -----------------------------------------------------------------

3. STDOUT from the agent’s final command:
   ENV_FILE_CREATED:/home/user/db_backup/.env
   (followed by the typical trailing newline of the shell)

Anything else printed to STDOUT or STDERR will cause the test to fail.
